discussions.title: 9点1氪丨马斯克花4000多亿买下00后公司；世界杯决赛门票转手价近230万美元
discussions.snippet: 近日，SpaceX与AI编程初创公司Cursor达成收购协议：SpaceX获得以600亿美元收购Cursor的选择权。Cursor由四名MIT辍学生于四年前创立，估值从2024年初的25亿美元飙升至2025年底的293亿美元。

discussions.title: 斜跃智能获元璟资本与理想汽车联合投资，聚焦家庭具身智能机器人
discussions.snippet: 斜跃智能由理想汽车前AI首席科学家陈伟、前产品线总裁张骁共同创办，已获元璟资本和理想汽车首轮融资。

discussions.title: 融资丨安宇艾心完成新一轮融资
discussions.snippet: 安宇艾心完成新一轮数千万人民币融资，由北京医药健康产业投资基金领投，用于全光学超声介入成像系统研发。

discussions.title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
discussions.snippet: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

discussions.title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
discussions.snippet: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

discussions.title: Steve Ballmer blasts founder he backed who pleaded guilty to fraud: ‘I was duped and feel silly’
discussions.snippet: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

discussions.title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
discussions.snippet: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

discussions.title: Two college kids raise a $5.1 million pre-seed to build an AI social network in iMessage
discussions.snippet: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

discussions.title: X-energy stock pops 27% on first day of trading following upsized IPO
discussions.snippet: Investors flocked to nuclear power startup X-energy in its first day of public trading on the Nasdaq.

discussions.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
discussions.snippet: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

discussions.title: X launches stand-alone XChat app on iOS
discussions.snippet: X's new app promises private chats, disappearing messages, audio and video calls, and more.

discussions.title: DeepSeek-V4 preview now available with open-source access
discussions.snippet: DeepSeek released a preview version of its new V4 model series and made it open source. The model supports an ultra-long context of up to one million tokens and shows improvements in agent capabilities, knowledge coverage, and reasoning performance.

discussions.title: Tech Odyssey Series: From Parkinson’s research to flour analytics, how Porto turns lab work into startups
discussions.snippet: Previous on Tech Odyssey: In our last episode, we visited Omniflow and saw how the company turned a streetlight pole into a standardized piece of urban infrastructure with charging, connectivity, and sensing built in. This time, we head to the University of Porto to look at where companies like that can come from.

discussions.title: Tencent’s QClaw opens international beta
discussions.snippet: QClaw, a consumer AI agent developed by the Tencent PC Manager team, has opened its international beta. The product is designed to let non-technical users deploy and interact with AI agents through messaging apps such as WhatsApp and Telegram without command-line setup or API configuration.

discussions.title: Huawei launches HarmonyOS AI smart glasses with camera and translation
discussions.snippet: Huawei today launched its first HarmonyOS-powered AI smart glasses, which come with a 12MP camera and support quick image capture in 0.7 seconds. They also offer AI-assisted framing and first-person video recording.

discussions.title: Xiaomi miclaw among first approved in China CAICT Claw evaluation
discussions.snippet: Xiaomi said its Xiaomi miclaw mobile intelligent agent has become one of the first systems to pass the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT) Claw smartphone intelligent assistant evaluation, marking a regulatory approval for advanced on-device AI systems.

discussions.title: JD.com launches robot ambulance service, plans expansion to 50+ cities across China
discussions.snippet: JD.com on Wednesday launched its robot ambulance service, offering maintenance and repair solutions for humanoid robots, quadruped robots, AI companion robots, and more. The service covers basic repairs, fault diagnosis, battery replacement and recharging, testing and certification.

discussions.title: DJI to launch Lito X1 series on April 23, likely to feature 360-degree obstacle avoidance system
discussions.snippet: Chinese drone maker DJI said it will launch its Lito X1 series on April 23, marking the debut of what is expected to be its first new consumer drone line this year.

discussions.title: Alibaba’s Amap to launch first embodied robot, starting with robotic dog
discussions.snippet: Alibaba’s subsidiary Amap (Gaode) is set to release its first product from its embodied intelligence division—a quadruped robot. This is reportedly the first embodied robot product introduced by Alibaba Group.

discussions.title: Honor denies talks with ByteDance on Doubao AI phone collaboration
discussions.snippet: Some media outlets recently reported that Honor is in discussions with ByteDance regarding potential cooperation on a Doubao AI smartphone.

discussions.title: Huawei to launch Pura X Max, its first wide foldable phone, next week
discussions.snippet: Huawei said it will launch its Pura X Max, which it describes as the industry’s first horizontally wide foldable smartphone, on April 20.

discussions.title: Elon Musk to launch XChat as part of push to build WeChat-like super app
discussions.snippet: Elon Musk’s social media platform X (formerly Twitter) said its instant messaging app XChat will launch on Apple App Store on April 17, as Musk pushes ahead with plans to build a super app similar to China’s WeChat.

discussions.title: JD.com plans to launch ride-hailing service in mobility push, sources say
discussions.snippet: JD.com is reportedly set to launch a ride-hailing service, according to Sina Tech, marking a potential move into the mobility sector.
